summary: Iteration 22: Tested News CRUD, News RSS, Navigation Manager, Blog Manager, Page Builder and dashboard auth persistence. Backend: 6/12 tests PASS. CRITICAL duplicate-route conflict discovered: /api/content/news POST is being handled by old /app/backend/routes/content.py:create_news which throws NameError: name 'user' is not defined (line 268 references undefined 'user' - the old handler has no auth dependency injecting the user). The newer /app/backend/routes/news.py router is registered AFTER content.py, so the broken old handler wins and ALL news create/update/delete via POST fail with HTTP 500. GET /api/content/news and /api/content/news/public return 200 but are served by content.py's list_news (different shape than new news.py). RSS /api/news/rss.xml and /api/news/sitemap.xml return 200 valid XML. Navigation endpoint not found at /api/navigation*. Frontend: login works, /dashboard/navigation-menu loads without logout (bug fix confirmed), /dashboard/news-manager, /dashboard/blog-manager, /dashboard/page-builder all load. News Manager empty state with Create News Article button renders correctly. Blog Manager Visual/HTML toggle buttons not visible on list page (they appear inside the edit/create modal - not verified).

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error: NameError: name 'user' is not defined at /app/backend/routes/content.py line 268 ('author_id': user['user_id']). Old content.py create_news has no `user` dependency - it shadows the new news.py POST handler due to registration order. News creation is COMPLETELY BROKEN via the UI.

Same duplicate-route conflict likely affects update/delete (couldn't verify because create fails first). Main agent must EITHER remove news endpoints from /app/backend/routes/content.py OR register news_router BEFORE content_router in server.py so the new correct handlers take precedence.

minor.issue: No navigation GET endpoint found at /api/navigation, /api/navigation/menu, or /api/navigation/items. Navigation router prefix/routes may differ - main agent to confirm the intended public read endpoint.

Returns 404 because the new news.py handler defines /slug/{slug} but the router registered after content.py has 'news/{news_id}' catch-all which may also cause shadowing once create works. Worth retesting after route conflict fix.

integration_issues.issue: UI will fail on save since backend POST /api/content/news returns 500 (duplicate route bug). Empty state UI renders correctly.
Could not visually verify ReactQuill Visual/HTML toggle - it is inside the edit modal (not opened in this test run). Main agent should self-verify by opening edit modal.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix duplicate /content/news route conflict. Recommended: DELETE the old news endpoints (create_news, list_news, and any other /news routes) from /app/backend/routes/content.py so the new /app/backend/routes/news.py router handles all /content/news/* requests.
Alternatively: reorder server.py so api_router.include_router(news_router) is called BEFORE api_router.include_router(content_router).
After fix, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_22.py - should go from 6/12 to 12/12.
Main agent to self-verify Blog Manager Visual/HTML editor toggle by clicking Edit on an existing blog post.
Confirm Navigation Manager's actual backend endpoint path (not at /api/navigation).
Confirm Page Builder 'New Page Type' dropdown exposes all 21 types (couldn't open the Create modal in test run).
critical_code_review_comments: Duplicate news endpoint definition across /app/backend/routes/content.py and /app/backend/routes/news.py is a code-quality red flag. One should be authoritative. Prefer keeping the new news.py (it has proper auth dependency, slug uniqueness, uuid news_id, and consistent shape) and removing old code from content.py.
content.py:create_news references an undefined `user` variable - indicates an unfinished refactor. Would also fail lint if strict mode were on.
news.py author_id/author fields vs content.py author_id/author field names are inconsistent - schema drift risk.

success_rate.backend: 50% (6/12 passed - all failures traced to one duplicate-route root cause)
success_rate.frontend: partial - login + routes load without logout verified; News create/edit modal flow blocked by backend bug; Blog Visual/HTML toggle and PageBuilder 21 page-types not opened in test run

seed_data_creation: None - attempted TEST_ prefixed news creation; rolled back by failure.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Root cause of News 500s is a duplicate FastAPI route registration - two handlers at /content/news. Once main agent removes the /news routes from content.py (keep the new news.py), rerun pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_22.py -v to confirm 12/12. Frontend auth-persistent navigation to /dashboard/* now works (sign-out bug fix confirmed). Remaining UI checks: Blog Visual/HTML toggle inside edit modal, Page Builder create-page dropdown should list 21 types, Content sidebar menu should show 7 items (observed: Page Builder, Blog Manager, News Manager = 3; need to confirm all 7).
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/content/news returns 500 with 'NameError: name user is not defined' because /app/backend/routes/content.py:254 defines @router.post('/news') create_news(data, request) with NO user dependency, yet line 268 references user['user_id']. Meanwhile /app/backend/routes/news.py:115 defines a correct @router.post('') create_news with user=Depends(require_admin). Because content_router is registered before news_router in server.py, FastAPI picks the broken content.py handler first. Fix: remove duplicate news endpoints from content.py (preferred) or swap include_router order. Reproduction: POST to /api/content/news with any body -> 500.
